subject: UX & Research
title: Time on Task
description: A 60-second lesson on Time on Task.

hook.text: Users took 90 seconds to find checkout. Last month it took 30. Something changed, and it's not good.

buildup.text: Time on task measures efficiency. For goal-driven tasks, faster is better. Set a target and measure against it.

discovery.text: Compare time across user groups. Experts at 20 seconds, novices at 3 minutes shows a learnability problem.

twist.text: For content sites, more time is good. A reader spending 8 minutes on an article is engaged, not struggling.

climax.text: Context decides meaning. Pair time data with task type: transactional tasks want speed, content wants depth.

punchline.text: Fast isn't always good. Match time to task type and context.

quiz.question: When is longer time on task actually a positive signal?
options: On content pages where more time means deeper engagement
On checkout flows where users browse payment options
On error pages where users read the message carefully
